hypothesis: If E2EFOLD is trained end-to-end with a composite loss that jointly optimizes structure reconstruction (FAPE, Cα-FAPE, torsion, distogram) and sequence recovery, then it will achieve higher sequence recovery and more accurate structure predictions (lower RMSD and higher TM-score) on benchmark datasets than models trained separately for structure prediction and sequence design, because the joint objective aligns sequence and structure optimization.
